other_observers: Maggie Boswell and John Vanderpoel.  Originally found by Steve Mlodinow.
description: An obvious goose, that was a little larger than the thousands of Cackling Geese it was with.  Darkish head, with a dark eye and a bicolored bill that was about 60% pink, but dark near the head and at the tip.  Brown back with thin white lines that were horizontal over the upper back but were along the feather edges along the lower back. Brownish on the sides of the upper breast with more white horizontal lines, but the rear half of the breast was pure white that reached back to the tail.  The bird was not seen in flight, but just sitting on the water.  When it swam, the upper part of the legs were pink, and this is shown better in the attached photo by Jim Esten that I downloaded from Cobirds. It is difficult to see from this photo, but Joe Mammoser, who saw the bird close up at the first location it was found, said the hallux on both legs were intact.
